rule: Purple stick: select farthest end from target, place in clear space, then use midpoint formula placed=2*target-other_end for exact landing.
meta: L1 is tutorial — single stick compass. Ball = midpoint of 2 endpoints.

rule: Two compasses: purple stick + orange tripod. Solve stick first (faster convergence). Tripod: ball = centroid of 3 ends, moves by displacement/3. Route around obstacles — ball jumps them, only landing matters.
meta: Obstacles block endpoint placement AND ball landing. But ball can fly over during animation.

rule: Green quadpod (4 legs) + purple stick. S-curve obstacle wraps center. Route quadpod endpoints RIGHT first (x>48), then DOWN through right-side gap in obstacle, then toward target. LEFT side has no safe passage.
meta: Quadpod centroid moves by displacement/4. S-curve upper arm spans full width at y=22-28. Must route through RIGHT-side gap.

rule: 3 compasses: purple+orange+green stick, red+blue stick, yellow+green tripod. Large center obstacle. Solve red+blue first (4 steps), purple second (6 steps), tripod last. Route tripod BELOW obstacle then UP on right side.
meta: Match balls to targets by shared color composition. Multicolored balls go to multicolored targets.

rule: Paradigm shift: white compass balls collect colors by landing on stationary colored balls. Ball absorbs color (visible as pixel change). Must collect ALL colors matching a multicolored target before delivering. White ball is a shopping cart.
meta: L5 changes the mechanic completely. Stationary colored balls + colored target rings. Green+yellow target needs both green AND yellow collected first.

description: Ball can fly over ALL obstacles during animation. Only the FINAL landing position is checked against obstacle collision sprites. Endpoint placement is also destination-only check. Path does not matter for either.
meta: Critical insight that turned impossible levels into straightforward solutions. Previous assumption that ball path was checked was WRONG.

description: White compass balls absorb color when landing on stationary colored balls. The ball's pixels change to show absorbed colors. Must collect ALL colors matching a target ring before delivering. Targets are multicolored (e.g., green+yellow ring needs both green AND yellow).
meta: L5+ mechanic. Subtle pixel change in the ball — must look AT the ball after landing, not at the stationary object.

description: LLM must track objects, endpoints, objectives, and predictions EVERY step. Without explicit tracking, intention amnesia causes drift — stating one plan then executing something unrelated on the next move. Format: objects list, current objective, planned action, predicted result, verify after.
meta: General principle applicable to ALL games. The #1 failure mode is losing track of what you're doing mid-sequence.
